title: Photosynthesis in green plants
text: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants and chloroplasts convert sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide into glucose and oxygen. Chlorophyll absorbs light energy to drive the light-dependent reactions.

title: Mitochondria and cellular respiration
text: Mitochondria are the powerhouse of the cell, producing ATP through cellular respiration. The electron transport chain across the inner mitochondrial membrane generates a proton gradient used by ATP synthase.

title: Black holes and event horizons
text: A black hole is a region of spacetime where gravity is so strong that nothing, not even light, can escape. The boundary is called the event horizon, and supermassive black holes sit at galactic centers.

title: Supernova stellar explosions
text: A supernova is the explosive death of a massive star, briefly outshining an entire galaxy. Core collapse supernovae forge heavy elements and scatter them across interstellar space.

title: Inflation and monetary policy
text: Inflation is the sustained increase in the general price level of goods and services. Central banks raise interest rates to cool demand and bring inflation back toward their target.

title: Government bonds and yields
text: A government bond is a debt security issued by a treasury to fund spending. The yield moves inversely to the price, and longer maturities usually carry higher yields to compensate for risk.

title: Photonics and optical fibers
text: Photonics studies the generation and control of photons, especially in optical fibers that carry internet traffic. Total internal reflection guides light down the glass core with very low loss.

title: Transistors in modern electronics
text: A transistor is a semiconductor device that amplifies or switches electrical signals. Billions of MOSFET transistors are etched onto a single silicon chip in modern microprocessors.

title: Antibiotics and bacterial resistance
text: Antibiotics kill or inhibit bacteria by targeting their cell walls or protein synthesis. Overuse drives antibiotic resistance, producing strains that no longer respond to common drugs.

title: Vaccines and immune memory
text: A vaccine trains the immune system by presenting a harmless piece of a pathogen. Memory B cells and T cells then mount a faster response on future exposure to the real infection.

title: Glaciers and climate change
text: Glaciers are slow-moving rivers of ice that store much of the planet's fresh water. Rising temperatures accelerate glacier melt, contributing to sea level rise and altered river flows.

title: Volcanoes and plate tectonics
text: A volcano forms where magma rises through the crust, often at plate boundaries or hotspots. Eruptions release lava, ash, and gases, and can reshape entire landscapes over time.
